evidence.work_arrangement: Apply remote type Hybrid locations Amsterdam, Netherlands
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Follow established best practices for data modelling, naming conventions, and platform governance
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD is dominated by governance, standardisation, quality checks, and peer review language with no speed/shipping framing, so rigour dominates.
evidence.domain_risk: Knowledge of the real estate sector is a plus but not required.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The employer is a commercial real estate services firm (Cushman & Wakefield) where BI outputs inform investment/valuation decisions, a business-decision-with-financial-stakes context treated as high domain risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: BI Development team; Data Science & Engineering
evidence.data_team_maturity: you will be an integral part of the Analytics Engineering pillar, contributing to the development and maintenance of the data foundation that powers BI delivery across EMEA
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The JD describes an established, multi-pillar EMEA BI function with senior Analytics Engineers, standards, bootcamps, and governance already in place, indicating a mature data team.
evidence.jd_authorship: Contribute to the design, build, and maintenance of semantic models and curated datasets on our central data platform, working under the guidance of senior Analytics Engineers.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The JD names specific platform concepts (semantic models, central data platform, enterprise tools like Databricks/Snowflake/Power BI) that a hiring manager would know, indicating direct hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: You will work in close collaboration with the BI and Data Science & Engineering teams
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role is oriented around the internal BI/data platform function itself rather than a specific external commercial, product, or finance stakeholder group.
evidence.autonomy_level: Contribute to the design, build, and maintenance of semantic models and curated datasets on our central data platform, working under the guidance of senior Analytics Engineers.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: As a junior role, nearly every responsibility is framed with supportive verbs (contribute, support, assist) under senior guidance rather than independent direction-setting.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Contribute to the design, build, and maintenance of semantic models and curated datasets on our central data platform
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The candidate joins an existing central data platform and BI pillar, extending and standardising it rather than building new infrastructure from scratch.
evidence.ai_role: Support early-stage work on building data foundations that can be used for AI and agentic use cases, such as preparing clean, well-structured datasets ready for future AI applications
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate helps build data foundations and clean datasets intended for downstream AI and agentic use cases, a clear ai_enabler signal even though the role is junior/supportive.
evidence.testing_framing: Contribute to data quality checks and basic performance monitoring; Support testing and quality assurance activities across project delivery
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and quality assurance are mentioned but framed with supportive verbs (contribute, support, assist) under senior guidance rather than full candidate ownership, closer to tool_listed than a true owned responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Contribute to data quality checks and basic performance monitoring of data models and semantic layers; ensure data assets and platform changes are reliable before release
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability and quality checks are present but framed as junior/supportive contributions secondary to delivery and learning, not a dominant compliance or trust narrative.
